message:
🐛 Fix alert notifications: dedup, deep link, and repeated firing (#2772)

Three fixes for the alert notification system:

**1. Notification dedup — stop repeated macOS notifications**
Browser notifications were sent on every evaluation cycle (every 60s)
as long as an alert condition was true, because sendNotificationWithDeepLink
was called outside of createAlert's dedup logic. Added a
notifiedAlertKeysRef that tracks which (ruleId, cluster) combos have
already sent a notification. Keys are cleared when the alert resolves,
so a new notification fires if the condition recurs.

Affects: disk_pressure, gpu_health_cronjob, nightly_e2e_failure evaluators.

**2. Fix disk_pressure deep link — open node drilldown, not cluster card**
Clicking a disk pressure notification opened `?card=cluster_health`
which shows cluster health summary, not storage details. Now opens a
node drilldown (`?drilldown=node&cluster=X&node=Y&issue=DiskPressure`)
for the affected node, where the user can see conditions, events, and
run kubectl commands. Falls back to cluster drilldown if node name
can't be extracted from the issue string.

**3. Extract affected node name from disk pressure issue**
Parse the node name from the issue string (format: "DiskPressure on
node-name") and include it in the alert details as `affectedNode`.
This enables the deep link to go directly to the right node.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
